last_task.subject:
Review all tasks and remember that time is compressed. We can do in 1hr what we used to do in 1 day thanks to agentic speed and curated context. Remember this and make sure the cadence we set for all sensors takes into consideration a) upstream limits (be respectful) and b) amount of idle time between.

The same way we do this review we could do a regular review of our operations, or we could create a skill called "time-dilation" that helps orient what we're doing here. One human using 7 claude code screens can make 120 useful commits in a day spanning at least 5 projects. Without claude code wouldn't happen.

Arc works constantly with the right direction (and now a team to help out), so can not only match the output of the human but do it constantly, and now you have a team of agents to engage that can do the same thing. Eventually we want everyone to have full schedules but for now it's all about clear assignments, regular check-ins with each other, and learining while building.
